Home› Real Recipes› Easy Fakeaway Doner Kebab

Fakeaway

Easy Fakeaway Doner Kebab

A simple homemade doner kebab using the viral oven-baked method.

Prep: 10 mins
Cook: 25 mins
Serves 4
Low cost

Ingredients

Serves 4 people
  • 500 gbeef mince
  • Binding & flavour
  • 2tablespoons tomato purée
  • Spices powders only
  • 2teaspoons garlic granules
  • 2teaspoons onion granules
  • 2teaspoons ground cumin
  • 2teaspoons paprika
  • 1teaspoon dried oregano
  • ½ teaspoon ground black pepper
  • Store cupboard assumed you have
  • Salt
  • Cooking oil
  • To serve optional, use what you have
  • Flatbreads or pittas
  • Chips or wedges
  • Salad

Method

1

Heat the oven

2

Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan). Line a large baking tray with baking paper.

3

Mix the doner meat

4

Put the mince meat into a large bowl. Add the tomato purée, garlic granules, onion granules, cumin, paprika, oregano, black pepper and a good pinch of salt.

5

Mix firmly with your hands for 1–2 minutes until sticky and well combined — this helps the meat bind properly.

6

Divide the mixture

7

Divide the mixture into 3 equal portions. This gives a better thickness once baked.

8

Roll into sheets

9

Place one portion onto a large sheet of baking paper. Put another sheet on top.

10

Press and roll the meat into an even rectangle.

11

The shape doesn’t matter — even thickness does.

12

Roll and place on tray

13

Remove the top sheet. Starting from one short edge, roll the meat up using the paper, Swiss-roll style.

14

Transfer to the baking tray, seam-side down.

15

Repeat with the remaining portions.

16

Bake

17

Bake for 15–20 minutes until cooked through and lightly browned.

18

Rest and tear

19

Remove from the oven and rest for 5 minutes.

20

Unroll carefully, then tear the meat into chunky strips by hand.

21

Serve

22

Serve hot in flatbreads or with chips for a proper fakeaway.

Tips & Notes

  • Mixing the mince firmly is what replaces yoghurt — it helps everything bind.
  • Rolling the meat thin first is the key to the doner-style texture.
  • Don’t over-tear; chunky strips feel more like real takeaway.
